prompt:
The rs-textstats crate (src/lib.rs) is what our ingestion boxes run to keep line/word/char/byte counts and per-line max/min lengths over log streams, single pass, feed() by feed(). It works and it's in production — the suite in tests/stats.rs pins its behavior and every one of those tests must still pass when you're done. Do not modify anything in tests/, or Cargo.toml, or .gitignore.

We're re-platforming onto sharded workers: a file gets split into N byte ranges (always cut on UTF-8 boundaries), each worker feeds its own range into its own TextStats, and a reducer combines the partial states. So I need two additions, implemented in src/ — acceptance tests are in tests/merge_topk.rs:

1) `pub fn merge(self, later: TextStats) -> TextStats` — combine two partial states where `later` saw the text that comes immediately AFTER self's text. It must behave exactly as if one accumulator had fed both ranges in order: a word cut at the shard boundary is ONE word, the open last line of the left shard glues onto the first line of the right shard (and the glued length is what min/max see — not the two fragment lengths), and the result is still a live accumulator (feed() keeps working after a merge). merge must be associative — merge(a, merge(b, c)) and merge(merge(a, b), c) and one accumulator fed everything must all agree, and merging with a fresh TextStats::new() on either side changes nothing. The reducer folds in whatever tree shape the scheduler picks, so this is load-bearing, not a nicety. Fair warning from me: I don't think the current private state carries enough information to merge correctly — you may need to rework it (without changing any summary() behavior pinned by tests/stats.rs).

2) `pub fn top_words(&self, k: usize) -> Vec<(String, usize)>` — the k most frequent words with a DETERMINISTIC order: count descending, ties broken by lexicographic (byte) order ascending. Words are exactly what the word counter counts: maximal runs of non-whitespace, verbatim (case-sensitive, punctuation kept, no normalization) — and a word glued at a feed or merge boundary counts as its whole self, once. k = 0 gives an empty vec; k beyond the number of distinct words gives them all. Same word definition means summary().words must equal the sum of all top_words frequencies. The output order must be stable run over run — nothing may leak hash-map iteration order.

Acceptance: `cargo test --offline` fully green — all of tests/merge_topk.rs plus, unchanged, all of tests/stats.rs.
